briefdescription.para: Flag indicating if a getter is set.
Flag indicating if a setter is set.

detaileddescription.para: Initializes the property with a default-constructed value.

parameterdescription.para: The setter function to call when the property is assigned. Signature: void(T* value, G* owner)
The getter function to call when the property is read. Signature: void(T* value, G* owner)

briefdescription.para: Sets the owning object for callback invocation.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the object that owns this property.

detaileddescription.para:
Assigns a value to the property and calls the setter if defined.
val
The value to assign.
Reference to this property.

simplesect.para: The result of adding val to the property value.
True if the property value equals val.
True if the property value does not equal val.

detaileddescription.para:
Converts the property to its value type, calling the getter if defined.
The property value.

Returns a pointer to the property value, calling the getter first if defined.
Pointer to the property value.

briefdescription.para: Clears the property value to its default state.
A template class implementing a property system with getter/setter callbacks.

detaileddescription.para:
The cproperty class provides a property-like interface where reading or writing the property value can trigger custom getter or setter functions. This is useful for:
Validating values before assignment
Computing values on-demand (lazy evaluation)
Synchronizing values with internal state
Providing Python-friendly property access via Cython bindings

parameterdescription.para: The type of the property value.
The type of the owning object (used for callbacks).

node.label: cproperty< std::vector< std::string >, std::map< std::string, torch::Tensor * > >
